---
title: "A Sample Data Analysis"
author: "Author"
date: "2026-04-09"
categories: [analysis, python]
description: "Demonstrating Python code execution and plots in Quarto."
---

```{python}
#| echo: true
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
x = np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, 200)
y = np.sin(x) + rng.normal(scale=0.1, size=x.shape)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
ax.plot(x, y, label="noisy sine")
ax.plot(x, np.sin(x), linestyle="--", label="true sine")
ax.set_xlabel("x")
ax.set_ylabel("y")
ax.legend()
ax.set_title("Sine wave with noise")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Summary statistics

```{python}
print(f"Mean:   {y.mean():.4f}")
print(f"Std:    {y.std():.4f}")
print(f"Min:    {y.min():.4f}")
print(f"Max:    {y.max():.4f}")
```

Quarto runs the Python kernel, captures outputs, and embeds them directly in the rendered HTML — no manual export needed.
